ccel.title: NPNF1-07. St. Augustine: Homilies on the Gospel of John; Homilies on the First Epistle of John; Soliloquies

source.title: NPNF1-07. St. Augustine: Homilies on the Gospel of John; Homilies on the First Epistle of John; Soliloquies

title: Chapter XV. 11, 12.

source.title: NPNF1-07. St. Augustine: Homilies on the Gospel of John; Homilies on the First Epistle of John; Soliloquies

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: You have just heard, beloved, the Lord saying to His disciples, “These things have I spoken unto you, that my joy might be in you, and that your joy might be full.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And what else is Christ’s joy in us, save that He is pleased to rejoice over us?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And what is this joy of ours which He says is to be made full, but our having fellowship with Him?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: On this account He had said to the blessed Peter, “If I wash thee not, thou shall have no part with me.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: His joy, therefore, in us is the grace He hath bestowed upon us: and that is also our joy.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But over it He rejoiced even from eternity, when He chose us before the foundation of the world.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Nor can we rightly say that His joy was not full; for God’s joy was never at any time imperfect.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But that joy of His was not in us: for we, in whom it could be, had as yet no existence; and even when our existence commenced, it began not to be in Him.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But in Him it always was, who in the infallible truth of His own foreknowledge rejoiced that we should yet be His own.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Accordingly, He had a joy over us that was already full, when He rejoiced in foreknowing and foreordaining us: and as little could there be any fear intermingling in that joy of His, lest there should be any possible failure in what He foreknew would be done by Himself.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Nor, when He began to do what He foreknew that He would do, was there any increase to His joy as the expression of His blessedness; otherwise His making of us must have added to His blessedness.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Be such a supposition, brethren, far from our thoughts; for the blessedness of God was neither less without us, nor became greater because of us.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: His joy, therefore, over our salvation, which was always in Him, when He foreknew and foreordained us, began to be in us when He called us; and this joy we properly call our own, as by it we, too, shall yet be blessed: but this joy, as it is ours, increases and advances, and presses onward perseveringly to its own completion.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Accordingly, it has its beginning in the faith of the regenerate, and its completion in the reward when they rise again.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Such is my opinion of the purport of the words, “These things have I spoken unto you, that my joy might be in you, and that your joy might be made full:” that mine “might be in you;” that yours “might be made full.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For mine was always full, even before ye were called, when ye were foreknown as those whom I was afterwards to call; but it finds its place in you also, when ye are transformed into that which I have foreknown regarding you.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And “that yours may be full:” for ye shall be blessed, what ye are not as yet; just as ye are now created, who had no existence before.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “This,” He says, “is my injunction, that ye love one another, as I have loved you.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Whether we call it injunction or commandment, both are the rendering of the same Greek word, entolé (ἐντολή).

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But He had already made this same announcement on a former occasion, when, as ye ought to remember, I repounded it to you to the best of my ability.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For this is what He says there, “A new commandment I give unto you, that ye love one another; as I have loved you, that ye also love one another.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And so the repetition of this commandment is its commendation: only that there He said, “A new commandment I give unto you;” and here, “This is my commandment:” there, as if there had been no such commandment before; and here, as if He had no other commandment to give them.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But there it is spoken of as “new,” to keep us from persevering in our old courses; here, it is called “mine,” to keep us from treating it with contempt.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But when He said in this way here, “This is my commandment,” as if there were none else, what are we to think, my brethren?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Is, then, the commandment about that love wherewith we love one another, His only one?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Is there not also another that is still greater,—that we should love God?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Or has God in very truth given us such a charge about love alone, that we have no need of searching for others?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: There are three things at least that the apostle commends when he says, “But now abide faith, hope, charity, these three; but the greatest of these is charity.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And although in charity, that is, in love, are comprehended the two commandments; yet it is here declared to be the greatest only, and not the sole one.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Accordingly, what a host of commandments are given us about faith, what a multitude about hope! who is there that could collect them together, or suffice to number them?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But let us ponder the words of the same apostle: “Love is the fullness of the law.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And so, where there is love, what can be wanting? and where it is not, what is there that can possibly be profitable?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The devil believes, but does not love: no one loveth who doth not believe.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: One may, indeed, hope for pardon who does not love, but he hopes in vain; but no one can despair who loves.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore, where there is love, there of necessity will there be faith and hope; and where there is the love of our neighbor, there also of necessity will be the love of God.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For he that loveth not God, how loveth he his neighbour as himself, seeing that he loveth not even himself?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Such an one is both impious and iniquitous; and he that loveth iniquity, manifestly loveth not, but hateth his own soul.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let us, therefore, be holding fast to this precept of the Lord, to love one another; and then all else that is commanded we shall do, for all else we have contained in this.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But this love is distinguished from that which men bear to one another as such; for in order to mark the distinction, it is added, “as I have loved you.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And wherefore is it that Christ loveth us, but that we may be fitted to reign with Christ?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: With this aim, therefore, let us also be loving one another, that we may manifest the difference of our love from that of others, who have no such motive in loving one another, because the love itself is wanting.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But those whose mutual love has the possession of God Himself for its object, will truly love one another; and, therefore, even for the very purpose of loving one another, they love God.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: There is no such love as this in all men; for few have this motive for their love one to another, that God may be all in all.
